title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Spiral Review
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a performance-based branch network capstone after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.
Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.
Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Net Host Cli Tools evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Net Host Cli Tools assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Host Cli Tools, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful ping proves DNS, transport ports, and the application are healthy. This fails the Net Host Cli Tools contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Zero Trust Architecture (NIST SP 800-207) — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.

hints: Name the Net Host Cli Tools precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful ping proves DNS, transport ports, and the application are healthy.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

stimulus.title: Net Certification Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives. Artifact under review: a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Net Certification Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Certification Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
